author: y4rr
content:
Landed GPTQ q4 + AWQ q4; HQQ split out to #613.

## What landed in \`ferrotorch-llama/src/quant_loaders.rs\`

### \`GptqQ4\` + \`dequantize_gptq_q4\`
HF auto_gptq layout (q4):
- \`qweight\`: \`[K/8, N]\` i32 (8 int4 weights packed per i32 along K)
- \`qzeros\`: \`[K/G, N/8]\` i32 (8 int4 zeros packed per i32 along N) — note GPTQ stores zero - 1, the unpacker reconstructs the true zero
- \`scales\`: \`[K/G, N]\` f32
- \`g_idx\`: optional \`[K]\` i32 permutation for \`act_order=True\` checkpoints

Output: \`[N, K]\` row-major f32 (matches torch's \`Linear.weight\` shape).

### \`AwqQ4\` + \`dequantize_awq_q4\`
HF autoawq layout (q4):
- Different packed layout: \`qweight\` is \`[K, N/8]\` (N is the packed axis, opposite of GPTQ)
- AWQ-specific channel shuffle in each i32: lanes pack out-channels in order \`[0, 4, 1, 5, 2, 6, 3, 7]\`. The runtime kernel exploits this for cache-friendly stripes; the unpacker explicitly inverts it.
- Same \`scales\` shape as GPTQ.

Same output shape as GPTQ: \`[N, K]\` row-major f32.

### Validation
Both unpackers do thorough shape / divisibility checks before reading any byte of input:
- in/out_features must be multiples of 8 (q4 packing)
- in_features must be a multiple of group_size
- All packed buffer lengths must match the declared shape
- \`g_idx\` length must equal in_features when present

## Tests (9 new)
- \`gptq_q4_dequantize_one_group_identity\` — synthesize known q values, verify the closed-form dequant (q - zero) × scale pattern
- \`gptq_q4_with_two_groups_uses_per_group_scale\` — same q across two groups with different scales → magnitude-scaled output per group
- \`gptq_q4_rejects_*\` — three error-path cases (non-multiple-of-8 dims, group misalignment, g_idx length mismatch)
- \`awq_q4_dequantize_uniform_inputs\` — every dequant lands at a single value (sanity)
- \`awq_q4_shuffle_order_is_distinct_from_gptq\` — pack 8 distinct lane values, verify they unshuffle to the AWQ \`[0, 4, 1, 5, 2, 6, 3, 7]\` order
- \`awq_q4_rejects_*\` — error paths

9 quant tests pass. \`cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --features cuda\`: zero regressions.

## Out of scope → #613
- HQQ (per-row half-precision scales, multiple bitwidths, different packed layout per bitwidth)
- Real-checkpoint integration tests (load \`TheBloke/Llama-2-7B-GPTQ\` from HF cache, verify forward-pass logits match)
- bitsandbytes NF4/FP4, ExLlamaV2, Marlin (most are GPTQ-derivatives)
